hero.title: Visual frontend development for real projects
hero.subtitle: Design interfaces, compose interactions, and manage code output in the browser. Prodivix moves ideas into a maintainable engineering workflow faster.

why.title: Visual development should stay connected to real engineering.

body.p1: Many visual tools are good at producing a first screen, but they become harder to trust once a project needs routes, components, state, resources, code, and deployment. The prototype and the implementation start to drift.
body.p2: Prodivix is not designed to hide code. It is designed to keep visual authoring, code authoring, and project output in the same workflow, so a project can begin visually and still become maintainable frontend software.

explanation.title: It treats page editing and project formation as one workflow.
explanation.subtitle: Prodivix is not just a canvas that exports a block of code. It is closer to a browser-based frontend development environment: interface, behavior, code, and output need to reference each other and remain understandable as the project grows.

state.label: Project model
state.title: The project has a stable structure
state.body: Pages, components, resources, and relationships belong to an explicit project model instead of living only in temporary canvas state. That gives editing, preview, and output the same source of context.

authoring.label: Authoring
authoring.title: Code remains part of authoring
authoring.body: When logic, styles, or external library adapters are needed, code should not become a hidden side effect. It should be editable, referenceable, diagnosable, and connected to visual editing.

output.label: Delivery
output.title: Output is shaped for delivery
output.body: The tool ultimately needs to serve frontend projects that can be built, deployed, and versioned. Prodivix organizes its editors around that outcome instead of only optimizing in-platform presentation.

workflow.title: From idea to delivery, the path should stay continuous.
workflow.subtitle: A homepage does not need to explain every internal architecture decision. What matters to the user is that each step keeps the same context instead of forcing the project to be rebuilt in another tool.

shape.title: Shape the interface
shape.body: Start with pages, components, and layout so an idea becomes something the team can evaluate quickly.

connect.title: Connect behavior
connect.body: Interactions, data flow, and logic should remain editable and traceable instead of disappearing into temporary state.

refine.title: Keep control
refine.body: As a project grows, developers still need to inspect, adjust, and understand the code behind the experience.

ship.title: Prepare to ship
ship.body: The output should serve real engineering: buildable code, deployment paths, and version control.

capabilities.title: One context for work that usually gets split apart.
capabilities.subtitle: The long-term value of Prodivix is not another canvas. It is fewer breaks between design, logic, code, and delivery.

interface.title: Interface and components
interface.body: Build pages, components, and layout visually while preserving a path into real implementation work.

logic.title: Interaction and logic
logic.body: Keep behavior, execution logic, and motion in an authoring model that can be understood and edited.

output.title: Code and delivery
output.body: A frontend project eventually needs code, builds, Git, and deployment. Prodivix is designed around that destination.

audience.title: It is for people who need prototypes to keep moving toward engineering.
audience.subtitle: If the goal is only a static presentation, many tools already work well. Prodivix is focused on what happens when a page begins to carry logic, code, dependencies, and delivery requirements.

builder.title: For builders who want a faster first interface
builder.body: Start visually, but keep a path into a real project structure as the work becomes more serious.

team.title: For product teams making shared decisions
team.body: Keep interface, interaction, and engineering feasibility in the same object of discussion, so less intent is lost between prototype and implementation.

developer.title: For developers who still need control
developer.body: Visual editing can increase speed, but the project should not become a black box. Code, output, and extension points still need to be explainable.

developer.title: Visual development should not remove developer control.
developer.subtitle: Mature frontend projects need explainability, portability, and extension points. Prodivix treats those engineering constraints as part of the product experience.

inspectable.title: Inspectable
inspectable.body: Important relationships between interface, logic, and code should be possible to locate, inspect, and explain.

portable.title: Portable
portable.body: Project output should not exist only for an in-platform preview. It should map to real project files and version control.

extensible.title: Extensible
extensible.body: When built-in capabilities are not enough, the project should still be able to use code, dependencies, and external libraries.

finalCta.title: Start with an editable frontend project.
finalCta.subtitle: Open the editor to try the current workflow, or read the documentation to understand how Prodivix organizes projects, editors, and output.

footer.description: Prodivix is a browser-based visual frontend development environment for keeping interface ideas connected to engineering delivery.
